name: Email Triage to Karbon Project Assignment
description: VA automation: triages Gmail emails to Karbon Work Items. Resolves real ClientKey via Organization/Contact email lookup before filtering open projects. PATCH appends AI notes to confident matches; POST creates a flagged review Work Item for uncertain ones

parameters.text:
=You are sorting an incoming email into the correct work item in our practice management system (Karbon). Here is the email and a list of this client's currently open projects.

EMAIL SUBJECT: {{ $json.subject }}
EMAIL BODY: {{ $json.body }}

OPEN PROJECTS FOR THIS CLIENT:
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

Rules:
- If the email clearly relates to one project, set confidence "high"
- If it could plausibly relate to a project but isn't explicit, set confidence "medium"
- If there's no clear match, set matched_project_id to null and confidence "low"
- suggested_category must be one of: Reply, Schedule, Decide, Reference

The matched_project_id, if set, must be the WorkItemKey of one of the open projects listed above.

options.systemMessage: You are an email triage classifier for a bookkeeping practice. You ONLY output structured JSON matching the required schema. Never include extra commentary.

parameters.jsonSchemaExample: {"matched_project_id":"WI-1023","confidence":"high","reasoning":"Email mentions November receipts, matching the Monthly Bookkeeping - Nov 2025 project.","suggested_category":"Reply"}

channelId.value: <__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Slack channel ID for AI-confident assignments, e.g. karbon-assignments__>
<__PLACEHOLDER_VALUE__Slack channel ID for manual review, e.g. karbon-needs-review__>

parameters.content:
## GOAL
Automatically triage incoming client emails for the bookkeeping practice. Triggers:
- Gmail polling (real-time, every minute)
- Morning Schedule at 7:00 AM daily
- Afternoon Schedule at 4:00 PM daily

Pipeline: Redact -> Lookup Client (Org or Contact) -> Get Open Projects -> AI Classify -> Patch Work Item + Slack confirm (confident) OR Create Review Work Item + Slack alert (uncertain).

## Karbon API Credential

Karbon requires TWO headers on every request:
1. Authorization: Bearer {token} -- set as the value in your Header Auth credential
2. AccessKey: {JWT} -- added manually as an extra header on each HTTP node (see placeholders)

Get both from: Karbon -> Settings -> Connected Apps -> API Applications -> Manage

Base URL: https://api.karbonhq.com/v3/

Use ONE Header Auth credential across all Karbon nodes -- do not create a new one per node.

## Client Lookup (NEW)

Karbon ClientKey is an opaque ID, not a domain. This step resolves it properly:

1. Search Organizations by EmailAddress eq <sender email>
2. If no match, search Contacts by EmailAddress eq <sender email>
3. Whichever matches first sets client_key + client_type

If neither matches, client_found is false and downstream nodes are warned via Slack rather than failing silently.

## Redaction Step

Strips SSNs, EINs, account/routing numbers, and card numbers before the AI sees anything. Tune the regex patterns against real email samples.

## VA Automation Schedule

Morning run: 7:00 AM -- start-of-day review
Afternoon run: 4:00 PM -- end-of-day review

Both schedule triggers feed the same redact/classify/route pipeline as the Gmail trigger.

## Important: No Notes/Triage API

Karbon's v3 API has no separate "Notes" or "Triage" resource. Confirmed via developer docs:

- 6a writes the AI note into the Work Item Description field via PATCH (the only partial-update field Karbon supports), after fetching the existing Description so nothing is overwritten.
- 6b creates a brand-new Work Item titled "NEEDS REVIEW: ..." assigned to alex@honeybearfinancials.com, since there is no API Triage inbox to drop items into.

Review these new Work Items in Karbon and reassign/merge as needed.

## Slack Channel Setup

Two channels, two purposes:

#karbon-assignments (suggested name)
Receives a checkmark message every time AI confidently assigns an email to a Karbon project. FYI only -- no action needed. Set in node 7a.

#karbon-needs-review (suggested name)
Receives a warning message when AI creates a new review Work Item assigned to alex@honeybearfinancials.com. Set in node 7b.

Use ONE Slack OAuth2 credential across both nodes -- do not create a new one per node.

## Duplicate Credentials Cleanup

Your n8n account has duplicate credentials of these types:
- httpHeaderAuth (3 found) -- keep ONE for Karbon
- slackOAuth2Api (2 found) -- keep ONE

Go to Settings -> Credentials in n8n, open each duplicate, check if it has real saved values, and delete the empty/unused ones. This workflow now references only one of each.
